manuscript_id.description: Primary YAML manuscript specialty for the ask turn.

suggested_capability_ids.description: Capability catalog ids the operator suggests for this ask.

oneOf.description: Native Liquid scene pinned to a custom surface. `config.scene` holds an opaque scene payload (`{ ops: [...] }`) the daemon never interprets — the client decodes + renders it via the Liquid SceneRenderer.

IngestRequest.title: IngestRequest
IngestRequest.description: Request from any channel adapter to the centralized ingester.

attachments.description: Optional attachment payloads merged into ask prompts

text.description: The text content of the message (command or prompt)

user_id.description: User identifier within the channel, e.g. "telegram:user:12345"

IngestAttachment.description: Optional attachment forwarded by a channel adapter.

is_new_session.description: Whether this is a brand-new session (first message or after /new)

job_id.description: If a job was enqueued, its id (for polling/streaming)

reply.description: Immediate text reply (help text, confirmation, error message)

session_id.description: The resolved or created session_id for this channel+user pair

stream_id.description: SSE stream id when a job-backed ask is processing

stream_ready.description: Whether the stream endpoint is ready for subscription

stream_url.description: Absolute URL for SSE stream consumption by adapters

identity_user_id.description: Optional identity principal override (debug/internal). Default: active workshop profile.

manuscript_id.description: YAML manuscript specialty for ranked digest + scheduled tool allowlist.

max_tool_rounds.description: When set, overrides `tui_defaults.json` for this turn (TUI live settings).

media_refs.description: User media uploaded to local medousa/media/ before this turn (P5a).

surface.description: Channel adapter context for ambient prompting (ingest/TUI surfaces).

voice_preset_id.description: Composer voice stance — short appendix block (not a manuscript specialty).

MediaRef.description: Reference to a user file stored locally under medousa/media/ (not inline bytes).

channel_surface.description: Adapter surface: telegram, discord, slack, home-desktop, home-ios, tui, api, …

supports_browser_host.description: When true, the connected client can run Agent Browser (local BrowserHost or client WebView). Telegram/TUI/ingest leave this false; Home desktop/iOS set true when browser is available.

supports_ui_artifacts.description: When true, the connected client can render sandboxed HTML UI artifacts (`cognition_ui_present`). Channel adapters and clients set this — the daemon does not infer it from channel name.

browser_challenge_url.description: URL the client should load in Agent Browser WebView.

browser_session_id.description: Agent Browser CAPTCHA / verification handoff session id.

budget_request_id.description: Turn budget approval pause — card id for Home deep link / notifications.

context_usage.description: Per-layer context budget estimate (chars/4 heuristic) for operator telemetry.

debug_message.description: Engine/TUI telemetry — shown only when the operator opts into engine details.

operator_message.description: Human-facing status whisper for rich surfaces (Home default).

previous_artifact_id.description: Previous artifact id when cognition_artifact_write supersedes a revision.

root_artifact_id.description: Root artifact lineage id for revision chains.

seq.description: Monotonic per-turn sequence number, stamped server-side by `TurnEventChannel::publish`. Enables exactly-once replay/dedup on reconnect. `#[serde(default)]` keeps the Python SDK and any older payloads (which never carried `seq`) wire-compatible.

tool_run_id.description: Structured tool bus (P1) — correlates started/finished pair.

ui_artifact.description: Rich UI artifact presented inline in chat (cognition_ui_present).

ui_scene.description: Liquid UI scene operations (cognition_ui_scene) — model-authored structure-then-fill turns. Ops are opaque JSON validated client-side.

work_id.description: Turn worker handoff — workspace card id (`work-…`).

ContextUsageLayer.description: One slice of the prompt/context budget (Cursor-style context usage UI).

ContextUsageReport.description: Turn-start context composition sent once per interactive turn.

context_limit_tokens.description: Model context window when known from capability registry / route.

StreamUiScene.description: Liquid UI scene channel payload — a batch of model-authored scene ops for one turn. `ops` are forwarded verbatim as opaque JSON (the client decodes and validates them into typed `SceneOp`s); the daemon never inspects their shape.

ops.description: Ordered scene operations, opaque JSON validated client-side.

surface_id.description: Scene surface id; the client defaults to `chat:{turn_id}` when absent.

turn_id.description: Owning turn id (stamped by the stream event builder).

McpGatewayStatusResponse.title: McpGatewayStatusResponse
McpGatewayStatusResponse.description: Daemon-proxied MCP gateway liveness + server runtime snapshot.

delivery.description: Optional channel push target for each successful materialized run.

display_name.description: Human-readable title for Automations list rows.

feeds.description: Optional environment feed ids to publish on each materialized run terminal.

manuscript_id.description: Optional YAML identity manuscript (loads task template, tool allowlist, pins).

oneOf.description: Ephemeral-style progress captured between tool rounds (not the final answer).
Forward-compatible catch-all for newer clients reading older persisted timelines.
Ephemeral-style progress captured between tool rounds (not the final answer).
Forward-compatible catch-all for newer clients reading older persisted timelines.

delivery.description: Replace delivery binding; pass `{ "delivery": null }` to clear channel push.

feeds.description: Replace feed binding; pass `{ "feeds": null }` to clear feed publish.

auto_workshop_tags.description: Merge default workshop + vault tags on write (default true).

session_id.description: Chat session id for workshop linking tags (`chat:…`, `session`, `profile:…`).

context_line.description: Secondary context: intent, tools, wrapping-up reasons.

detail_line.description: Operator-facing task label (full prompt when user_ack is a slug).

intent.description: Worker intent or job family at emit time.

tool_names.description: Tools invoked on the work card when the event was emitted.
